title: Marine Specialist
description:
Key Responsibilities

Operations & Safety

  • Ensure safe operation of barges & vessels in compliance with company policies, flag state, and international regulations (e.g., SOLAS, MARPOL, ISM).
  • Monitor voyage planning, cargo operations, mooring, towing, and stability.
  • Conduct risk assessments and enforce safety management systems (SMS).
  • Investigate incidents, near misses, and non-conformities; implement corrective actions.

Maintenance & Technical Oversight

  • Oversee preventive and corrective maintenance programs.
  • Ensure hull, ballast systems, cargo systems, and deck equipment are maintained and operational.
  • Coordinate dry-dockings, repairs, and inspections.
  • Review maintenance reports and defect lists.

Crew Management

  • Supervise barge masters, mates, and deck crews.
  • Ensure crew certification, training, and competency requirements are met.
  • Support performance management and disciplinary actions when required.

Regulatory Compliance

  • Ensure compliance with class society, port state, and client requirements.
  • Prepare for and participate in audits and inspections.
  • Maintain vessel documentation and certification validity.

Budget & Planning

  • Assist with budgeting for maintenance, repairs, and operations.
  • Control costs while maintaining safety and reliability.
  • Support fleet planning and asset utilization.

Stakeholder Coordination

  • Liaise with charterers, terminals, shipyards, surveyors, and regulatory bodies.
  • Coordinate with marine operations, HSQE, and technical departments.

Qualifications & Experience

  • Master Mariner, O.I.M, Marine warranty surveyor or equivalent maritime qualification (preferred).
  • 5–10+ years of experience in barge or offshore marine operations.
  • Strong knowledge of marine regulations and safety management systems.
  • Experience with audits, inspections, and dry-docking.
  • Excellent leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills.

Work Environment

  • Combination of office-based and field work.
  • Frequent travel to vessels, ports, and shipyards.
  • May involve irregular hours and on-call responsibilities.

postedCompany.description: Crystal Offshore is a recognized one-stop logistics solution provider to the Marine & Offshore Industry.  We serve Oil majors, Drilling contractors, Service companies, FPSO operators, Offshore Fabricators, Rig Building and Conversion yards.  Our offshore support service division owns and manages various types of offshore support vessels for charter.  We provide complete agency, logistics, warehousing, procurement, engineering / fabrication services, design and supply of integrated fire and safety systems.
postedCompany.name: CRYSTAL OFFSHORE PTE. LTD.
